Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, Cation-dependent O-methyltransferase family
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. Cation-dependent O-methyltransferase family. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=a catechol + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = a guaiacol + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:17877, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:33566, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:134251; EC=2.1.1.6; Evidence={ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:P21964};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Catalyzes the O-methylation, and thereby the inactivation, of catecholamine neurotransmitters and catechol hormones (By similarity). Required for auditory function. Component of the hair cell's mechanotransduction (MET) machinery. Involved in the assembly of the asymmetric tip-link MET complex. Required for transportation of TMC1 and TMC2 proteins into the mechanically sensitive stereocilia of the hair cells. The function in MET is independent of the enzymatic activity (PubMed:28534737). {ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:P21964, ECO:0000269|PubMed:28534737}.

Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Catalyzes the O-methylation, and thereby the inactivation, of catecholamine neurotransmitters and catechol hormones (PubMed:18794526). Required for auditory function (PubMed:18794526, PubMed:28504928). Component of the cochlear hair cell's mechanotransduction (MET) machinery. Involved in the assembly of the asymmetric tip-link MET complex. Required for transportation of TMC1 and TMC2 proteins into the mechanically sensitive stereocilia of the hair cells. The function in MET is independent of the enzymatic activity (PubMed:28504928). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:18794526, ECO:0000269|PubMed:28504928}.

Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=a catechol + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = a guaiacol + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:17877, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:33566, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:134251; EC=2.1.1.6; Evidence={ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:A1Y9I9}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:17878; Evidence={ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:A1Y9I9};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Catalyzes the O-methylation, and thereby the inactivation, of catecholamine neurotransmitters and catechol hormones (By similarity). Required for auditory function (PubMed:18794526). Component of the cochlear hair cell's mechanotransduction (MET) machinery. Involved in the assembly of the asymmetric tip-link MET complex. Required for transportation of TMC1 and TMC2 proteins into the mechanically sensitive stereocilia of the hair cells. The function in MET is independent of the enzymatic activity (By similarity). {ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:A1Y9I9, ECO:0000269|PubMed:18794526}.

Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Catalyzes the O-methylation, and thereby the inactivation, of catecholamine neurotransmitters and catechol hormones. Shows highest activity towards catecholestrogens and dobutamine. Also has lower activity towards L-DOPA, dopamine and epinephrine. Active towards the xenobiotic compounds methyl-DOPA, carbidopa, isoproterenol, and apomorphine. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:21371608}.

Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Catechol O-methyltransferase that can use various catechol-like compounds (PubMed:33596655). Can produce vanillic acid (meta-form) and iso-vanillic acid (para-form) from protocatechuic acid (PCA) (PubMed:33596655). Does not have a regiospecificity, and produces the meta- and para-forms of the products in equal proportion (PubMed:33596655). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:33596655}.

Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Catechol O-methyltransferase that can use various catechol-like compounds such as gallic acid (GA), 3,4-dihydroxy-5-methoxy-benzoic acid (5OMeBA), protocatechuic acid (PCA), 3,4-dihydroxy-benzaldehyde (DHA), dopamine, caffeic acid (CA), luteolin, quercetin, and 5-hydroxyuridine. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:31147608}.
